sources.reason: User brief names dwarkesh.com Substack `/p/` as primary free path #5. Verified via direct curl 2026-05-17: 200 OK, 693KB HTML, inline `Andrej Karpathy` / `Dwarkesh Patel` speaker labels, 9 H2 section markers with `(HH:MM:SS)` timestamps. No bot protection. RSS feed at /feed has 20 items, no podcast:transcript tags (transcript ships inline in /p/ HTML).
sources.notes: Substack-hosted; inline `Speaker` labels with H2 timestamps. HTML scrape via Surf-compatible direct HTTP, no challenge.

sources.reason: User brief names Podcasting 2.0 `<podcast:transcript>` namespace tag as source #6. Spec verified at podcasting2.org: required attributes url+type, MIME types text/vtt, application/x-subrip, text/html, application/json, text/plain. Probed two reference feeds (Dwarkesh and Huberman public RSS) — neither advertises podcast:transcript (Huberman has no podcast: namespace at all, Dwarkesh has 20 items with 0 transcript tags). Source remains valuable for the long-tail indie podcasts that do advertise transcripts.
sources.transcript_runtime: direct HTTP fetch of transcript URL from RSS, MIME-routed parser
sources.notes: Coverage for the 6 target shows is low (most don't advertise it); but source is cheap to implement and high-leverage for shows that do.

sources.reason: User brief names yt-dlp subprocess as source #7 with `--write-auto-subs --sub-langs zh-Hans,en --skip-download` for Xiaojun Chinese + auto-translate. yt-dlp 2026.03.17 confirmed installed locally. Pattern is production-tested via last30days-skill/scripts/lib/youtube_yt.py (per `reference_remotion_mastery_workspace` / `last30days-skill` memory).
sources.notes: Covers Xiaojun (zh-Hans + en translate), Huberman free path (every episode on YouTube), Acquired free path, generic YouTube. Subprocess install required on user machine; doctor command must check.

sources.reason: User added mid-run 2026-05-17. Spotify Premium ($11.99/mo) covers most non-member podcast content via auto-generated transcripts. Web app fetches transcripts via internal endpoint (~spclient.wg.spotify.com/transcript-read-along) using user's logged-in cookie + derived Bearer token. ToS-grey but user-side risk only (mirrors the 4 member publishers).
sources.transcript_runtime: Spotify Premium cookie capture (via `auth login --chrome --service spotify`) + replay against internal transcript endpoint
sources.notes: v0.1 ships the metadata slot, dispatcher route, and `auth login` integration; the HTML/JSON transcript parser ships in v0.2 because the endpoint shape requires first-time capture from a Premium-subscriber session. The endpoint has been refactored historically and is less stable than Substack HTML; we'll calibrate the parser against captured traffic. Expected coverage uplift: ~3-4 of the 6 target shows move from 'partial' to 'covered' for FREE episodes once Spotify is implemented.

sources.reason: User brief names spoken.md as paid universal path #8. Full API contract verified 2026-05-17 via spoken.md/: `GET https://spoken.md/search?q=`, `GET https://spoken.md/transcripts/{id}`, `x-api-key: pt_demo` demo key works without signup, Content-Type text/markdown, X-Credits-Charged + X-Credits-Remaining headers, output uses canonical `**Speaker** (MM:SS)` markdown. Pricing $0.08-0.15/transcript.
sources.transcript_runtime: direct HTTP, returns canonical markdown — no normalization needed
sources.notes: Smallest+highest-leverage paid adapter. Demo key reaches public episodes; production key for power user.

sources.reason: User brief names Taddy GraphQL bulk path as source #9. Endpoint shape verified via taddy.org/developers: GraphQL, X-USER-ID + X-API-KEY headers, `getEpisodeTranscript(uuid)` returns `[{id, text, speaker, startTimecode, endTimecode}]`. Free tier covers podcast-provided transcripts only (<1% of podcasts); paid tier Pro $40/mo for 100 transcripts.
sources.notes: Lower priority than spoken.md but valuable for bulk feed-sync use cases.

sources.reason: User brief names per-minute audio transcription #10 via OpenAI / Deepgram / ElevenLabs (ElevenLabs Scribe preferred for diarization + $0.004/min). Provider-pluggable. Last-resort fallback when no other source has the transcript.
sources.transcript_runtime: yt-dlp audio extract -> provider upload -> diarized transcript
sources.notes: Highest cost; behind --provider whisper or --auto-paid. No keys set on this machine currently — Phase 5 will smoke this only if user provides one.
